\usepackage{listings}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{calc}

\definecolor{codebg}{RGB}{248,248,248}
\definecolor{framecolor}{RGB}{221,221,221}

% Fix for Dimension too large error - reset maxwidth before each listing
\makeatletter
\let\original@lsthk@InitVarsEOL\lsthk@InitVarsEOL
\def\lsthk@InitVarsEOL{%
  \global\lst@maxwidth=0pt
  \original@lsthk@InitVarsEOL
}
\makeatother

\lstset{
  breaklines=true,
  breakatwhitespace=false,
  breakindent=0pt,
  breakautoindent=true,
  % Set safe linewidth using calc package
  linewidth=\linewidth-20pt,
  columns=fullflexible,
  keepspaces=true,
  showstringspaces=false,
  upquote=true,
  backgroundcolor=\color{codebg},
  frame=single,
  rulecolor=\color{framecolor},
  framesep=5pt,
  framerule=0.5pt,
  basicstyle=\ttfamily\footnotesize,
  keywordstyle=\color{blue},
  commentstyle=\color{gray},
  stringstyle=\color{orange},
  numbers=left,
  numberstyle=\tiny\color{gray},
  stepnumber=1,
  numbersep=8pt,
  captionpos=b,
  aboveskip=10pt,
  belowskip=10pt,
  xleftmargin=10pt,
  xrightmargin=10pt,
  % Add line continuation marker
  postbreak=\mbox{\textcolor{gray}{$\hookrightarrow$}\space}
}
